title: Tabernaemontana glabra
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana glabra is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Tabernaemontana, which is a group of flowering plants known for their complex alkaloids and medicinal properties. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. Members of the Apocynaceae family are found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. The classification of Tabernaemontana glabra reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and genus.
uses: Tabernaemontana glabra is known for its medicinal properties in various traditional medicine systems. It is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Tabernaemontana glabra is used to treat conditions such as rheumatism and gastrointestinal problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits and to develop its uses in modern medicine.

intro: Tabernaemontana glabra, also known as the smooth tree tabernaemontana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of T. glabra include alkaloids, such as vincamine and vincristine, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, T. glabra is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into ointments and other topical preparations.
